items.caption: 3 Things Professional Cleaners Should NEVER Do
items.transcript: It is free advice that you would never do it in a client's home. Okay, number three is when you move things, just move things to clean here, but put it back. And when you put it back, just be careful. Of course, this kind of things can happen, for sure not. Sorry for that. Okay, again, again. Okay, and the number three, how I was saying, just put them back to the place and that's it. So just people, they like to keep things in the same place. You don't need to move it, you move it, you put it back and that's it. Number two is, for example, when you go and people houses, people always they have money around. They put money, for example, here, or to just simple things like this, like here. You can do this kind of things just to see if you're going to take them off. But there is some clients and they do this kind of things that they hide the money. Another day we find, this is very funny, we find a money like this and the client left the book. And when we were cleaning, it came out and we say, oh, wow, we find $60. Oh, wow, so happy. So my team is very honest, you know, so they call me and they say, hi, hi, Alejandra, the client left $60 here for us. I say, $60 for you? I don't think so. Let me call the client to see if he's going to give you this for tips. And I call the client and say, hello, my friend, you left $60 here in the book. This is for my team. Just saying, I want to know if they can take it or no. And the client say, oh, no, no, no, leave it there. I say, okay. But this is the kind of thing that you say, oh, my God, what is this, you know? And the number one, and then it's very important. I forgot. The number one, please, when you go to people's houses, doesn't matter if you find a place super dirty, disgusting, horrible, don't say anything. You can't say anything because if you start saying things, people, they cannot understand what you're saying. It doesn't matter if it's Spanish, Chinese, but with your faces, they can see. So this is very important. Just respect every client, every house. It's very important to respect everything because we just go there to work. We don't go there to judge anybody. We just want to help everybody to get like a fresh cleaning and things like that. But this is very important. Just don't say anything. We're going to do our job. This is what we used to do. And that's it.

items.caption:
This client gave us a $300 tip. ❤️

When she first called us, she said, “I’m so sorry. My house is in very bad condition.”

I told her, “Please don’t worry. We’re not here to judge you. We’re here to help.”

When we arrived, we saw that the house needed a lot of work. One member of our team spent 7 hours cleaning just one bathroom. She was very tired, but she wanted to leave it perfectly clean.

The kitchen, the oven, and every corner of the house needed a deep cleaning.

Little by little, everything became clean again.

When we finished, the client started crying.

She cried when she saw the bathroom.
She cried when she saw the kitchen.
She cried when she saw the oven.

She hugged us and said she was so happy.

She told us she never wanted her home to get like that again and that she would keep calling us to help take care of it.

Before we left, she gave us a $300tip.

For us, it wasn’t about the money.

It was about making someone happy again and knowing that our work made a real difference.

Sometimes, cleaning a home is about much more than cleaning. Sometimes, it’s about giving someone a fresh start.

❤️ Thank you for trusting us.

Follow us for more cleaning transformations and real stories from Canada.

items.caption:
✨ N’oubliez pas de nous contacter pour l’entretien de votre immeuble ou de vos bureaux!

Nous nous occupons de tout le nettoyage afin que vous puissiez vous concentrer sur ce qui compte le plus.

🏢 Entretien commercial professionnel
🧼 Service fiable et minutieux
📍 Service offert dans la région de Montréal

ALLE NETTOYAGE – La propreté, notre priorité.

Have you ever wondered why a deep cleaning takes so much time?

Many people ask why a deep cleaning costs more and takes several hours. The answer is simple: because we clean the details that are usually overlooked.

A deep cleaning is much more than making a home look clean. It’s about removing hidden dust, dirt, grease, bacteria, and buildup from places most people never think about.

We clean window tracks, frames, baseboards, corners, and hard-to-reach areas where dust and cobwebs collect. We move furniture whenever possible, vacuum under beds, and clean behind decorations and plants to make sure every space is truly clean.

When we clean balconies, we first vacuum away dust, sand, leaves, and debris before washing every surface. We also remove and wash window screens, cleaning behind them before putting them back in place.

In the kitchen, we clean cabinets, countertops, drawers, backsplashes, and appliances with great attention to detail. We clean inside the refrigerator and oven, and vacuum behind and underneath large appliances to remove years of hidden dust.

Bathrooms receive one of the deepest cleanings. We remove soap scum, hard water stains, mineral buildup, and grime while sanitizing showers, bathtubs, sinks, faucets, toilets, mirrors, tiles, and grout until everything shines.

We dust ceiling fans, vacuum carpets and rugs, and thoroughly vacuum and mop every floor. Every room is carefully inspected to ensure no detail has been missed.

Finally, we sanitize the entire home, leaving it fresh, clean, and ready to enjoy.

A real deep cleaning isn’t about cleaning faster—it’s about cleaning better. It takes patience, attention to detail, professional techniques, and treating every home as if it were our own.

That’s why a true deep cleaning takes time.

Because excellence is found in the details.

And when we’re finished, the difference isn’t just something you can see—it’s something you can truly feel.

items.caption:
People always ask us, “What do you actually do during a cleaning?”

It’s more than just vacuuming and mopping. We clean the corners people forget, move plants and small items, remove dust behind them, clean window sills and tracks, and pay attention to every detail.

A great cleaning takes time, care, and dedication.

Follow us for more cleaning tips!
